Let me first check the credentials I have available, then run the actual end-to-end test with the user's login, and capture screenshots.

Let me understand the user's request. They want:

1. Test end-to-end IVX IA chat
2. Create a welcome banner "Welcome to IVX" that shows for 5-10 seconds then disappears
3. They want pictures (screenshots)
4. They want IVX IA to do the test, not me

Let me check the credentials I have available and the live state. Let me check the test creds file first since user said to use them.

output.stdout:
import json, urllib.request, ssl

ctx = ssl.create_default_context()

with open('expo/.env') as f:
    env = {}
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:
            k,v = line.split('=',1)
            env[k] = v.strip('"').strip("'")

# Test GitHub
token = env.get('GITHUB_TOKEN','')
print(f"GITHUB_TOKEN length: {len(token)}")
if token:
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/user', 
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: {resp.status} — {data.get('login','?')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: ERROR — {e}")

# Test repo access
if token:
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/repos/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest',
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: {resp.status} — {data.get('full_name','?')} (private={data.get('private')})")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: ERROR — {e}")

# Test Render
rk = env.get('RENDER_API_KEY','')
sid = env.get('RENDER_SERVICE_ID','')
print(f"\nRENDER_API_KEY length: {len(rk)}, SERVICE_ID length: {len(sid)}")
if rk and sid:
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://api.render.com/v1/services/{sid}',
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {rk}', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"RENDER: {resp.status} — {data.get('name','?')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"RENDER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"RENDER: ERROR — {e}")

# Test Supabase
su = env.get('SUPABASE_URL','')
sk = env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY','')
print(f"\nSUPABASE_URL length: {len(su)}, KEY length: {len(sk)}")
if su and sk:
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://{su}.supabase.co/rest/v1/',
        headers={'apikey': sk, 'Authorization': f'Bearer {sk}'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        print(f"SUPABASE: {resp.status}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"SUPABASE: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"SUPABASE: ERROR — {e}")

1. Goes to the chat app
2. Logs in with the provided credentials
3. Captures a welcome banner that shows "welcome to ivx" then disappears after 5-10 seconds
4. Sends a chat message
5. Captures the AI reply
6. Takes screenshots

1|//
     2|//  ChatTabView.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Mirrors the Android Chat tab (expo/app/(tabs)/chat.tsx):
     6|//  IVX Owner AI room, support tickets with "Create New Ticket",
     7|//  and the empty tickets state.
     8|//
     9|
    10|import SwiftUI
    11|
    12|struct ChatTabView: View {
    13|    var body: some View {
    14|        NavigationStack {
    15|            ScrollView {
    16|                VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 20) {

    18|                        Text("Chat")

1|//
     2|//  ContentView.swift
     3|//  Ivx
     4|//
     5|//  Root tab layout — exact mirror of the Android app's tab bar
     6|//  (expo/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx): all 7 tabs visible in the bottom bar
     7|//  (Home, Invest, Market, Portfolio, Chat, Profile, CRM), gold active
     8|//  tint #FFD700, inactive #777777, black bar with #242424 top border,
     9|//  and Chat as the initial tab (Android initialRouteName="chat").
    10|//
    11|//  A custom bar is required because a native SwiftUI TabView collapses
    12|//  tabs 5–7 into a "More" menu, which would not match Android.
    13|//
    14|
    15|import SwiftUI
    16|
    17|/// The 7 tabs, in the exact order the Android app declares them.
    18|enum IVXTab: Int, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
    19|    case home
    20|    case invest
    21|    case market
    22|    case portfolio
    23|    case chat
    24|    case profile
    25|    case crm
    26|
    27|    var id: Int { rawValue }
    28|
    29|    var title: String {
    30|        switch self {
    31|        case .home: return "Home"
    32|        case .invest: return "Invest"
    33|        case .market: return "Market"
    34|        case .portfolio: return "Portfolio"
    35|        case .chat: return "Chat"
    36|        case .profile: return "Profile"
    37|        case .crm: return "CRM"
    38|        }
    39|    }
    40|
    41|    /// SF Symbol equivalents of the Android lucide icons.
    42|    var icon: String {
    43|        switch self {
    44|        case .home: return "house"
    45|        case .invest: return "chart.line.uptrend.xyaxis"
    46|        case .market: return "chart.bar"
    47|        case .portfolio: return "briefcase"
    48|        case .chat: return "message"
    49|        case .profile: return "person"
    50|        case .crm: return "square.grid.2x2"
    51|        }
    52|    }
    53|}
    54|
    55|struct ContentView: View {
    56|    /// Android's Tabs initialRouteName is "chat" — start there too.
    57|    @State private var selectedTab: IVXTab = .chat
    58|
    59|    private let inactiveColor = Color(ivxHex: 0x777777)
    60|    private let borderColor = Color(ivxHex: 0x242424)
    61|
    62|    var body: some View {
    63|        TabView(selection: $selectedTab) {
    64|            HomeView()
    65|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    66|                .tag(IVXTab.home)
    67|
    68|            InvestView()
    69|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    70|                .tag(IVXTab.invest)
    71|
    72|            MarketView()
    73|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    74|                .tag(IVXTab.market)
    75|
    76|            PortfolioView()
    77|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    78|                .tag(IVXTab.portfolio)
    79|
    80|            ChatTabView()
    81|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    82|                .tag(IVXTab.chat)
    83|
    84|            ProfileTabView()
    85|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    86|                .tag(IVXTab.profile)
    87|
    88|            MembersView()
    89|                .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar)
    90|                .tag(IVXTab.crm)
    91|        }
    92|        .safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom, spacing: 0) {
    93|            customTabBar
    94|        }
    95|        .preferredColorScheme(.dark)
    96|        .background(Color.ivxBackground)
    97|    }
    98|
    99|    /// Bottom bar mirroring Android: black background, 0.5pt #242424 top
   100|    /// border, 10pt semibold labels, gold active / #777777 inactive.
   101|    private var customTabBar: some View {
   102|        HStack(spacing: 0) {
   103|            ForEach(IVXTab.allCases) { tab in
   104|                Button {
   105|                    selectedTab = tab
   106|                } label: {
   107|                    VStack(spacing: 3) {
   108|                        Image(systemName: tab.icon)
   109|                            .font(.system(size: 20, weight: .medium))
   110|                            .frame(height: 24)
   111|                        Text(tab.title)
   112|                            .font(.system(size: 10, weight: .semibold))
   113|                            .lineLimit(1)
   114|                            .minimumScaleFactor(0.8)
   115|                    }
   116|                    .foregroundStyle(selectedTab == tab ? Color.ivxGold : inactiveColor)
   117|                    .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, minHeight: 48)
   118|                    .contentShape(Rectangle())
   119|                }
   120|                .buttonStyle(.plain)
   121|                .accessibilityLabel(tab.title)
   122|                .accessibilityAddTraits(selectedTab == tab ? [.isSelected] : [])
   123|            }
   124|        }
   125|        .padding(.top, 6)
   126|        .padding(.bottom, 2)
   127|        .background(Color.ivxBackground.ignoresSafeArea(edges: .bottom))
   128|        .overlay(alignment: .top) {
   129|            borderColor.frame(height: 0.5)
   130|        }
   131|    }
   132|}
   133|
   134|#Preview {
   135|    ContentView()
   136|}

(End of file - total 136 lines)
